BRD:
Business Requirements Document (BRD)
1. Introduction
1.1 Purpose of this Document

This Business Requirements Document (BRD) outlines the business need, objectives, and high-level scope for the "Multi-Jurisdictional AIF Transfer Agent Workflow" feature within the AIF Regulatory Transfer Agent Copilot platform. It serves as a foundational document for all subsequent development phases, ensuring alignment between business goals and the technical solution. This document adheres to the BABOK v3 'Strategy Analysis' and 'Business Analysis Planning & Monitoring' knowledge areas.

1.2 Scope of this Document

This document focuses on defining the business problem, desired outcomes, key stakeholders, and the high-level functional and non-functional requirements for the automated handling of Alternative Investment Fund (AIF) investor transfers across multiple regulatory jurisdictions. It does not delve into detailed functional specifications (which will be covered in a subsequent Functional Requirements Document - FRD) or technical design.

1.3 Target Audience

This BRD is intended for:

Project Sponsors and Business Owners
Product Management
Business Analysts
Development Team Leads and Architects
Quality Assurance Team
Legal and Compliance Teams
Key Stakeholders involved in the AIF transfer process
2. Business Need
2.1 Problem Statement

The current process for handling AIF investor transfers is largely manual, highly complex, and prone to errors, particularly when dealing with multiple jurisdictions (e.g., India SEBI, EU AIFMD, US Reg D). This complexity is exacerbated by:

Cross-border KYC/AML checks: Varying requirements across regions lead to significant manual effort and potential compliance gaps.
Dynamic fee recalculations: Fluctuations in Net Asset Value (NAV) and diverse fee structures require intricate, often manual, calculations.
Real-time regulatory filings: Strict deadlines and specific formats for regulatory bodies (e.g., Form 15CA/CB) demand timely and accurate
